resources.string: Keeps local Nightscout loopback endpoint active for AAPS integration
Copilot keeps local AAPS/xDrip data channels alive
Shows whether AI optimizer tuning is currently active, stale, or blocked and explains why it was applied or disabled.
AI report and AI chat are activated after at least 24 hours of glucose history. This reduces noise and improves factor reliability.
Collecting data: %1$.1fh/%2$dh. AI report will unlock after minimum coverage.
Data window ready: %1$.1fh/%2$dh. You can run AI analysis and chat.
Chat uses local context: glucose, predictions, IOB/COB, ISF/CR, DIA, UAM, activity, steps, and replay quality metrics. It is advisory only.
AI chat is locked until minimum data window is collected (%1$.1fh/%2$dh).
No chat messages yet. Ask about trends, risks, or what drives forecast errors.
Example: Why is 60m MARD high today?
Record a question, transcribe it with OpenAI, and optionally hear the reply.
Recording is active. Tap stop to transcribe and send.
Processing voice input or generating spoken reply.
Explain the glucose trend for today
How do real ISF and CR compare now?
Did UAM affect the forecast today?
Cloud analytics backend disabled (OpenAI chat mode)
%1$dm • %2$s: |err| %3$s | pred %4$s vs actual %5$s | COB %6$sg | IOB %7$sU | UAM %8$s | CIw %9$s | activity %10$s
%1$dm • H%2$02d: worse %3$s | ΔMAE %4$s | ΔMARD %5$s | driver %6$s | n=%7$d
%1$dm mini-chart, %2$d points, last MAE %3$s, last MARD %4$s
Compensation-derived line currently matches Copilot fallback in this window.
Selected window is wider than available ISF/CR history, chart shows all available data.
Solid line: compensation-derived evidence. Dashed line: Copilot fallback runtime. Dotted line: AAPS raw values.
Priority for analytics and runtime interpretation: Compensation-derived evidence → Copilot fallback runtime → AAPS raw.
Copilot fallback coverage in selected window: %1$s%%
No compensation-derived ISF/CR points available in selected window.
No Copilot fallback ISF/CR points available in selected window.
No AAPS ISF/CR series found for selected window.
%1$s chart. Latest compensation-derived %2$s %3$s, Copilot fallback %4$s %3$s, AAPS raw %5$s %3$s.
Overlay lines are auto-scaled independently inside the visible window so COB, UAM and activity stay readable on top of ISF/CR.
%1$dm pair %2$s×%3$s %4$s×%5$s • meanA %6$s • meanB %7$s • MAE %8$s • MARD %9$s • Bias %10$s • n=%11$s
%1$dm top miss %2$s • err %3$s • pred %4$s • actual %5$s • COB %6$sg • IOB %7$sU • UAM %8$s • CIw %9$s • DIA %10$sh • activity %11$s • sensorQ %12$s
%1$dm %2$s cluster %3$s:00 • MAE %4$s • MARD %5$s • Bias %6$s • COB %7$sg • IOB %8$sU • UAM %9$s • CIw %10$s • driver %11$s • n=%12$s
%1$dm %2$s gap %3$s:00 • ΔMAE %4$s • weekday %5$s • weekend %6$s • ΔMARD %7$s • COB %8$sg • IOB %9$sU • UAM %10$s • CIw %11$s • driver %12$s • n=%13$s
Sensor lag replay by wear age
Best replay gain: %1$dm %2$s • raw %3$s -> lag %4$s • gain %5$s • n=%6$s
No positive lag-corrected replay gain in the current sample.
Highest SHADOW sensitivity: %1$s • rule change %2$s • mean abs target delta %3$s • n=%4$s
%1$dm %2$s • raw MAE %3$s • lag MAE %4$s • gain %5$s • raw bias %6$s • lag bias %7$s • n=%8$s
%1$s • rule change %2$s • mean abs target delta %3$s • n=%4$s
Weighted 30/60m gain %1$s with shadow sensitivity %2$s over %3$s replay rows. This bucket looks strong enough to validate for ACTIVE.
Weighted 30/60m gain %1$s with shadow sensitivity %2$s over %3$s replay rows. Keep this bucket in SHADOW until the gain is more stable.
Weighted 30/60m gain %1$s with shadow sensitivity %2$s over %3$s replay rows. Current replay does not justify rollout beyond observation.
Not enough replay rows yet for a bucket-level rollout call. Collect more local replay data before changing mode.
Current runtime state for sensor-lag correction, plus latest replay guidance for the current wear bucket when available.
Sensor-lag correction is disabled in settings.
Sensor-lag correction is currently ACTIVE for this sensor.
Sensor-lag correction is currently running in SHADOW for this sensor.
Sensor-lag correction is configured but waiting for the next eligible runtime cycle.
No replay guidance yet. Run daily analysis to populate bucket-level rollout signals.
No replay guidance yet for %1$s. Run daily analysis to evaluate this wear bucket.
24h trail: latest runtime points from the last 24 hours
Need at least 2 points for a 24h trend.
%1$s over the last 24 hours, %2$d points, latest %3$s.
Current replay signal is strong enough to validate ACTIVE for this wear bucket.
ACTIVE is already selected in settings for sensor-lag correction.
This wear bucket is not an ACTIVE candidate yet. Keep collecting SHADOW/replay evidence first.
ACTIVE is currently gated by runtime state: %1$s
SHADOW keeps raw forecasts and actions unchanged while logging the corrected path for comparison.
No adjacent bucket guidance available yet.
No adjacent bucket guidance yet around %1$s.
Not enough adjacent bucket data yet for a direct comparison.
%1$s looks stronger than adjacent buckets: current gain %2$s vs adjacent avg %3$s, shadow sensitivity %4$s vs %5$s.
%1$s looks weaker than adjacent buckets: current gain %2$s vs adjacent avg %3$s, shadow sensitivity %4$s vs %5$s.
%1$s is broadly in line with adjacent buckets: current gain %2$s vs adjacent avg %3$s, shadow sensitivity %4$s vs %5$s.
No shadow activation gate events yet
CR gate: gap ≤ %1$s min • sensor-blocked ≤ %2$s%% • UAM ambiguity ≤ %3$s%%
No dropped evidence data in selected windows
Rolling KPI is not available yet. Run daily analysis to populate 14d/30d/90d windows.
No wear impact diagnostics in selected windows
ISF hour-window evidence is below minimum
CR hour-window evidence is below minimum
ISF same day-type evidence is sparse
CR same day-type evidence is sparse
ISF day-type base profile is missing
CR day-type base profile is missing
ISF sample insulin is too small
ISF sample has no baseline glucose
ISF sample has no future glucose window
ISF sample has no positive glucose drop
ISF sample result is out of range
ISF sample window quality is too low
CR sample carbs are too small
CR sample has no nearby meal bolus
CR sample has too few glucose points
CR sample quality is too low
CR sample has too few valid intervals
Profile chart shows active insulin action over time, derived from cumulative model curves.
Insulin profile chart. Active %1$s. Horizon %2$s minutes.
X-axis: time since bolus. Y-axis: active insulin action level (%% of peak).
Peak active action: %1$s/5m at %2$s min, profile horizon %3$s min.
Real daily profile: updated %1$s • confidence %2$s • samples %3$d • status %4$s
Shape: onset %1$s min • peak %2$s min • scale %3$s
Reference profile is a solid line. Real calculated profile is shown as dashed overlay.
Real daily profile is not ready yet. Showing template profile.
Status includes icon and text for accessibility.
UAM cards display both inferred and calculated values.
Kill switch affects automatic actions only. Manual commands remain available.
Base endpoint for cloud AI analysis and reports
Secret token used for AI API authentication
Switch visual style only. Data sources, calculations and chart values remain unchanged.
Kill switch is enabled: automatic actions are blocked.
Kill switch is disabled: automatic actions are allowed.
This parameter controls the selected setting behavior and safety limits.
Current glucose card shows latest glucose, recent change speed and how fresh the sample is. These values drive the near-term risk checks.
Shows 5m, 30m and 60m forecasts with confidence interval. Wide interval means higher uncertainty and more conservative automation behavior.
UAM status combines detected unannounced impact and inferred carbs estimate. Higher UAM usually means hidden meal or stress-related rise is active.
Telemetry chips show IOB, COB, activity ratio and steps used by forecasting and safety layers. Missing chips reduce confidence.
Last action shows most recent temp target or carbs command and delivery status. Failed status means outbound channel needs attention.
Select how much history is shown before now. Longer range helps pattern review; shorter range helps minute-by-minute inspection.
Layer toggles control chart decomposition visibility: trend, therapy, UAM, and confidence band.
Chart combines historical glucose and future path forecast up to 60 minutes. The shaded band is the confidence interval around the prediction.
Horizon chips summarize forecast values for 5m, 30m and 60m including low/high confidence bounds.
Decomposition splits 60m effect into trend, announced therapy and UAM contributions plus debug residual metrics.
Summary compares inferred UAM carbs and calculated UAM branch values. Confidence indicates how reliable the estimate is for export and control.
Event list shows detected UAM episodes with carbs, confidence, mode and anti-duplicate state. Use actions to confirm, merge, or export.
Global hard limits control stale-data threshold, action rate, target bounds, and local Nightscout transport safety state.
Cooldown section shows temporary blocks and remaining timers that prevent too frequent automatic actions.
Checklist verifies critical guards (data freshness, sensor quality, limits). Any failed item means automation should be treated as constrained.
This block contains audit records for \"%1$s\": source, status, and details used to debug delivery and rule execution.
This section \"%1$s\" summarizes model quality and factor impacts. Use it to understand why forecasts change and where errors come from.
Enable kill switch and block automatic actions?
Disable kill switch and allow automatic actions?
Not enough points to draw chart
Forecast chart. Current glucose %1$s mmol/L. Forecast 60m %2$s mmol/L.
Only core controls: data sources, UAM, adaptive controller.
Detailed tuning: real ISF/CR engine, debug, retention and expert parameters.
Receive local glucose and therapy broadcasts
Primary Nightscout endpoint for sync and command delivery
Detect unannounced meals from glucose patterns
Increase inferred UAM sensitivity and impact
Allow inferred carbs export through AAPS transport
Calculate realtime ISF/CR without forcing it into control loops
Minimum confidence (%) to use realtime ISF/CR in runtime
Apply steps/activity ratio as short-term ISF/CR modifier
Apply stress/illness/hormonal tags in ISF/CR context model
Min ISF evidence per hour window
Required ISF evidence samples in local ±1h window before evidence blending
Min CR evidence per hour window
Required CR evidence samples in local ±1h window before evidence blending
Hard-drop CR evidence if max gap in meal window exceeds this value (minutes)
Hard-drop CR evidence if sensor-blocked telemetry share exceeds this limit (%)
CR gate max UAM ambiguity rate
Hard-drop CR evidence if UAM-ambiguity telemetry share exceeds this limit (%)
Automatically switch shadow mode to active when KPI gates pass
History window (hours) used for activation KPI
Minimum shadow diff samples required for activation decision
Required mean confidence (%) across shadow samples
Maximum mean absolute ISF delta (%) vs legacy profile
Maximum mean absolute CR delta (%) vs legacy profile
Required mean quality score (%) from realtime ISF/CR diagnostics in activation window
Required mean sensor factor (%) before SHADOW→ACTIVE promotion
Maximum mean wear confidence penalty (%) allowed in activation window
Maximum share (%) of diagnostics windows marked as sensor-age-high
Sensor gate max suspect false-low rate
Maximum share (%) of diagnostics windows marked as suspect false-low
Required mean ratio (%) of same-day-type evidence in hour windows before SHADOW→ACTIVE
Maximum share (%) of hour windows flagged as day-type sparse
Block shadow→active unless daily 24h quality limits are satisfied
Block SHADOW→ACTIVE when daily ISF/CR quality risk level is equal or above this threshold (2=MEDIUM, 3=HIGH)
Minimum matched points in latest 24h forecast report
Maximum allowed share (%) of glucose points below 3.9 mmol/L in last 24h
Minimum required CI coverage (%) for 30m horizon in last 24h
Minimum required CI coverage (%) for 60m horizon in last 24h
Maximum allowed mean CI width (mmol/L) for 30m horizon in last 24h
Maximum allowed mean CI width (mmol/L) for 60m horizon in last 24h
Minimum rolling windows (14d/30d/90d) that must pass for SHADOW→ACTIVE
Multiplier for MAE limits in rolling gates (%)
Multiplier for CI coverage lower bounds in rolling gates (%)
Multiplier for CI width upper bounds in rolling gates (%)
Severity used for next quick physiology tag
Duration used for next quick physiology tag
Log export actions without sending remote commands
Automatically adjust temp target from forecasts
Desired steady-state glucose target used by controller
Global lower safety bound for automatic temp target (4.0–10.0 mmol/L)
Global upper safety bound for automatic temp target (4.0–10.0 mmol/L)
Record extended diagnostics in local logs
How many days are stored for analytics and recalculation
Address where Copilot reads and sends data. If the URL is wrong, sync and commands will fail.
Base URL of the AI backend used for analysis jobs and reports. Wrong URL will break AI Analysis sync.
API key for AI backend access. Keep it private; invalid key will cause authorization errors.
Changes only the app appearance layer. Forecasts, ISF/CR, UAM and all safety logic keep the same inputs and outputs.
Runs a local Nightscout endpoint on the phone. Useful for local AAPS connection without an external server.
Reads glucose and therapy from Android broadcasts. If disabled, only Nightscout sync is used.
Accept data only from trusted sender packages. Improves safety, but unknown senders are blocked.
Detects likely unannounced carbs from glucose behavior. This influences UAM, forecasts, and safety decisions.
Makes UAM detection more sensitive. It reacts faster, but can overestimate carbs more often.
Allows sending inferred carbs to AAPS/Nightscout with backdated time. This can change COB and loop behavior.
Test mode: shows what would be sent without actually sending commands.
OFF: no export. CONFIRMED_ONLY: one export after confirmation. INCREMENTAL: send portions as the estimate grows.
Minimum carbs per inferred event. Higher value prevents tiny corrections.
Maximum carbs per inferred event. Safety cap against overly large inferred meals.
Rounding step for inferred carbs. Larger step means fewer, but coarser updates.
Use weekday/weekend historical glucose patterns as a bounded forecast prior
Adds a bounded time-of-day prior to the local forecast. It does not replace IOB, COB, UAM, ISF, or CR.
Main history window used for the stable weekday/weekend template. Longer windows are steadier but adapt slower.
Recent days that can softly shift the stable template
Short recency window adds bounded correction to the stable pattern. This keeps adaptation responsive without letting short noise dominate.
Build separate weekday and weekend templates with fallback to all days
Useful when weekends behave differently from workdays. If data is sparse, the engine automatically falls back to all days.
Apply small hour/day-type replay error correction on top of the circadian template
Uses past forecast-vs-actual signed error by time of day as a small bounded correction for 30 and 60 minutes.
Shows how much the circadian prior improves or worsens 30m and 60m MAE in recent local replay windows, split by all rows, calm windows, weekdays, and weekends.
Maximum share of the 30-minute forecast that may be shifted by circadian prior
Higher weight makes 30-minute forecasts follow historical time-of-day patterns more strongly. Acute states still attenuate this weight.
Maximum share of the 60-minute forecast that may be shifted by circadian prior
Higher weight makes 60-minute forecasts rely more on weekday/weekend pattern templates. Use conservatively if behavior changes quickly.
Enables automatic temp target control from forecasts to move glucose toward Base target.
Main glucose target for controller. Lower target is more aggressive; higher target is more conservative.
Insulin action curve (start, peak, tail). It directly affects IOB and prediction shape.
Lower hard bound for automatic temp target. Raising it adds hypo protection.
Upper hard bound for automatic temp target. Lowering it limits permissive high targets.
Threshold used for post-hypo safety logic. Helps detect risky rebound situations.
Safety target used after hypo/rebound pattern. Higher value lowers repeat-hypo risk.
Shows advanced debug metrics in UI (for example uci0, sigmaE, kfSigmaG, residualRoc0).
Writes detailed internal logs for diagnostics. Useful for debugging, increases local log volume.
How long history is kept for analytics and model calibration. Longer history improves pattern analysis.
Real ISF/CR is calculated in parallel and compared to legacy behavior before full activation.
Minimum confidence required to use real ISF/CR in runtime. Higher threshold is safer but adapts slower.
Applies steps/activity as a short-term sensitivity modifier. During load it usually raises hypo safety.
Uses manual stress/illness/hormonal tags in ISF/CR context model.
Minimum valid ISF samples around current hour before blending with baseline profile.
Minimum valid meal CR samples around current hour before blending with baseline profile.
If a meal window has too large CGM gaps, this CR sample is discarded as unreliable.
Discards CR meal samples when sensor-blocked telemetry share is too high.
Discards CR meal samples when UAM ambiguity is too high.
No circadian pattern data yet. Run analytics after at least 5 days of glucose history.
View weekday, weekend, and fallback templates by history window. These curves are bounded priors, not direct therapy commands.
Stable %1$dd • coverage %2$d days • confidence %3$s • quality %4$s • fallback active
No usable %1$s circadian points for this segment.
Circadian median glucose curve. Latest median %1$s.
Expected delta by time of day
No high-risk windows in this segment.
